```{r}
rows_base <- data_all %>%
  transmute(
    Year    = as.integer(.data[["Year"]]),
    Code    = as.character(.data[["Code"]]),
    Title   = as.character(.data[["Title"]]),
    Artist  = as.character(.data[["Artist"]]),
    Genre   = as.character(.data[["Genre"]]),
    Age     = as.character(.data[["Age category"]]),
    Country = as.character(.data[["Country"]]),

    Lowest_full_price = parse_number(as.character(.data[["Lowest full price"]])),
    Performances      = parse_number(as.character(.data[["Performances #"]])),

    First_date        = as.Date(.data[["First performance date"]]),
    Last_date         = as.Date(.data[["Last performance date"]]),

    Venue         = as.character(.data[["Venue"]]),
    Venue_code    = str_squish(as.character(.data[["Venue code"]])),   
    Venue_address = as.character(.data[["Venue address"]]),
    Venue_post    = as.character(.data[["Venue postcode"]]),
    Venue_site    = as.character(.data[["Venue website"]]),
    Venue_desc    = as.character(.data[["Venue description"]]),
    Venue_access  = as.character(.data[["Venue accessibility"]]),

    Latitude      = suppressWarnings(parse_number(as.character(.data[["Latitude"]]))),
    Longitude     = suppressWarnings(parse_number(as.character(.data[["Longitude"]])))
  )

# -----------------------------
# 2) 2025 Venue Dates (Date(s))
# -----------------------------
venue_dates_2025 <- rows_base %>%
  filter(Year == 2025, !is.na(Venue_code), Venue_code != "") %>%
  group_by(Venue_code, Venue) %>%
  summarise(
    first_date = safe_min_date(First_date),
    last_date  = safe_max_date(Last_date),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(`Date(s)` = mapply(fmt_range, first_date, last_date) |> as.character()) %>%
  select(Venue_code, `Date(s)`)

# -----------------------------
# 3) Website Venue table (remove activity level; insert Date(s))
# -----------------------------
venue_table_2025_for_site <- rows_base %>%
  filter(Year == 2025, !is.na(Venue_code), Venue_code != "") %>%
  group_by(Venue_code, Venue) %>%
  summarise(
    Address      = first_nonblank(Venue_address),
    Postcode     = first_nonblank(Venue_post),
    Website      = first_nonblank(Venue_site),
    Performances = sum(Performances, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Address  = str_squish(as.character(Address)),
    Postcode = str_squish(as.character(Postcode)),
    Website  = fix_url(Website),
    Performances = ifelse(is.infinite(Performances), NA_real_, Performances)
  ) %>%
  left_join(venue_dates_2025, by = "Venue_code") %>%
  transmute(
    Venue,
    Address,
    Postcode,
    `Date(s)`,
    Performances,
    Website
  ) %>%
  arrange(Venue)

# -----------------------------
# 4) Export xlsx
# -----------------------------
write_xlsx(
  list(Venue_Table = venue_table_2025_for_site),
  file.path(out_dir, "2025_venue_table_for_site.xlsx")
)
```

## NEW PLOT ADDED IN WEBSITE

## Concentration plot: Gov and Researcher
What: The first concentration chart reports the Herfindahl–Hirschman Index (HHI) each year, computed from each venue’s share of total performances; 
the Lorenz chart plots, for each year, the cumulative share of performances against the cumulative share of venues after sorting venues from smallest to largest. 

Why: Together they quantify how unevenly Fringe activity is distributed across venues—higher HHI and a more bowed Lorenz curve indicate that a small number of venues account for a large fraction of performances, which is useful for diagnosing market concentration and comparing how this imbalance changes from 2022 to 2025. 

How: We parse Year, Venue code, and Performances, aggregate to venue year totals, convert to venue shares within each year, then compute HHI = sum(share^2) and build Lorenz points via cumulative sums of sorted venue shares (with the 45° line as equality), plotting all years with different colours.

```{r}
#Concerntration 
parse_int <- function(x){
  suppressWarnings(as.integer(readr::parse_number(as.character(x))))
}
first_nonblank <- function(x){
  x <- x[!is.na(x) & stringr::str_trim(x) != ""]
  if (length(x)) x[1] else NA_character_
}
venue_perf_year <- data_all %>%
  transmute(
    Year       = as.integer(.data[["Year"]]),
    Venue_code = as.character(.data[["Venue code"]]),
    perf       = parse_int(.data[["Performances #"]])
  ) %>%
  filter(Year %in% years, !is.na(Venue_code), Venue_code != "") %>%
  mutate(perf = ifelse(is.na(perf) | perf < 0, 0L, perf)) %>%
  group_by(Year, Venue_code) %>%
  summarise(perf = sum(perf, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

hhi_year <- venue_perf_year %>%
  group_by(Year) %>%
  mutate(
    total = sum(perf, na.rm = TRUE),
    share = ifelse(total > 0, perf / total, 0)
  ) %>%
  summarise(HHI = sum(share^2, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(Year)

p_concentration_hhi <- ggplot(hhi_year, aes(x = Year, y = HHI)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = years) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = number_format(accuracy = 0.001)) +
  labs(
    title = "Venue concentration (HHI of performance shares)",
    x = "Year", y = "HHI"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

print(p_concentration_hhi)
```

```{r}
#Concentration（Lorenz）

lorenz_all <- venue_perf_year %>%
  group_by(Year) %>%
  arrange(perf, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    perf = pmax(perf, 0),
    total_perf = sum(perf, na.rm = TRUE),
    share = ifelse(total_perf > 0, perf / total_perf, 0),
    cum_share_perf = cumsum(share), #sum performance share
    n = n(),
    cum_share_venues = row_number() / n #Venues share
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

p_concentration_lorenz_all_years <- ggplot(
  lorenz_all,
  aes(x = cum_share_venues, y = cum_share_perf, colour = factor(Year), group = Year)
) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, linetype = 2) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = percent) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent) +
  labs(
    title = "Lorenz curve of venue performances (2022–2025)",
    x = "Cumulative share of venues",
    y = "Cumulative share of performances",
    colour = "Year"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

print(p_concentration_lorenz_all_years)

gini_by_year <- lorenz_all %>%
  group_by(Year) %>%
  summarise(
    gini = {
      x <- c(0, cum_share_venues)
      y <- c(0, cum_share_perf)
      area <- sum((x[-1] - x[-length(x)]) * (y[-1] + y[-length(y)]) / 2)
      1 - 2 * area
    },
    .groups = "drop"
  )

gini_by_year
```

##Venue VS price

Why：To see whether bigger venues (more total performances) tend to charge higher or lower typical ticket prices, and whether this relationship changes from 2022 to 2025; 

Who: For readers who want a quick view of venue market structure (researchers, reviewers, and users planning shows); 

How: Aggregate the raw 2022–2025 XLSX data by year × venue_code to compute performances_total = sum(performances), unique_events = n_distinct(code), and median_price = median(lowest_full_price), then plot a scatter with x = performances_total, y = median_price, point size = unique_events, and facet by year.

```{r}
venue_year_size_price <- data_all %>%
  transmute(
    year = as.integer(.data[["Year"]]),
    venue_code = stringr::str_squish(as.character(.data[["Venue code"]])),
    code = stringr::str_squish(as.character(.data[["Code"]])),
    perf = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(gsub("[^0-9.]+", "", as.character(.data[["Performances #"]])))),
    price = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(gsub("[^0-9.]+", "", as.character(.data[["Lowest full price"]]))))
  ) %>%
  filter(
    year %in% years,
    !is.na(year),
    !is.na(venue_code), venue_code != "",
    !is.na(code), code != ""
  ) %>%
  group_by(year, venue_code) %>%
  summarise(
    performances_total = sum(perf, na.rm = TRUE),
    unique_events = n_distinct(code),
    median_price = suppressWarnings(stats::median(price, na.rm = TRUE)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    median_price = ifelse(is.finite(median_price), median_price, NA_real_),
    performances_total = ifelse(is.finite(performances_total), performances_total, NA_real_)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(performances_total), performances_total > 0, !is.na(median_price))

p_vsize_price <- ggplot(venue_year_size_price,
                        aes(x = performances_total, y = median_price, size = unique_events)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.65) +
  facet_wrap(~ year, scales = "free_x") +
  labs(
    title = "Venue size vs ticket price (by year)",
    x = "Total performances per venue",
    y = "Median lowest full price",
    size = "Unique events"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

print(p_vsize_price)
```

## Ticket price distribution by year:

Why: Compare how ticket prices shift and spread across years (typical level, variability, and outliers) to check if prices are rising or becoming more uneven; 

Who: For anyone evaluating pricing patterns over time (users, researchers, reviewers); 

How: Parse lowest_full_price as numeric, keep non-missing values, and draw a year-by-year boxplot where the box shows the IQR, the line shows the median, and points indicate outliers.

```{r}
# =========================
# 10) Ticket price distribution by year — ggplot only (MATCH WEBSITE)
# =========================

price_box_df <- data_all %>%
  transmute(
    year = factor(as.integer(.data[["Year"]])),
    price = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(gsub("[^0-9.]+", "", as.character(.data[["Lowest full price"]]))))
  ) %>%
  filter(
    as.integer(as.character(year)) %in% years,
    !is.na(year),
    !is.na(price)
  ) %>%
  mutate(year = factor(as.integer(as.character(year)), levels = years))

p_price_box <- ggplot(price_box_df, aes(x = year, y = price)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.alpha = 0.25) +
  labs(
    title = "Ticket price distribution by year",
    x = NULL, y = "Lowest full price"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

print(p_price_box)
```
